text: Laparoscopic colectomy for T-for-B sigmoid colon carcinoma invading the bladder Laparoscopic surgery for PT-for-colon cancer is safe and feasible Any oncological outcomes are acceptable The aim of this video is to demonstrate a laparoscopic treatment for T-for-B sigmoid colon cancer invading part of the bladder

The patient was a fifty-six-year-old female who presented abdominal pain and hematochesia for two months.

The operative time was one hundred thirty-five minutes, with an estimated blood loss of forty milliliters, and the post-operative hospital stay was seven days.

The surgical system elevates the inferior mesenteric artery, while the peritoneum is incised from the mesangial root of the right sigmoid colon, exposing the space beneath. The space is then extended further.

The surgeon mobilizes the physiological adhesion of the sigmoid colon from the lateral abdominal wall, separating the colon from the surrounding tissue.

The surgeon incises the mesorectum, creating a separation approximately ten centimeters from the distal end of the tumor, revealing the surrounding tissue structures.
